publications.title: Deep peptide recognition profiling decodes TCR specificity and enables disease-associated antigen discovery.
publications.authors: Wang N, Yeh H, Lai B, Perera J, Jude KM, Risch I, Um J, Chen X, Xiang X, Wang C, Liu LD, Yang X, Paley MA, Khan AA, Garcia KC.
publications.executive_summary: This study employs high-throughput yeast display coupled with protein language models to generate deep peptide recognition profiles for HLA-B*27:05-restricted TCRs from patients with ankylosing spondylitis and acute anterior uveitis, revealing that these TCRs recognize their cognate peptides predominantly through CDR3β interactions. The trained models achieve superior prediction of T cell activation compared to existing structural prediction tools and identify novel candidate autoantigens relevant to these HLA-B*27:05-associated autoimmune diseases. The work demonstrates that TCR generalization depends on functional rather than sequence-based similarity, establishing a scalable platform for decoding TCR specificity and discovering disease-linked autoantigens—particularly valuable for understanding autoimmune pathology and informing rational TCR engineering approaches.
